Title: The mechanism by which oriented polypropylene packaging alleviates postharvest ‘Black Spot’ in radish root (Raphanus sativus)

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA esters to the corresponding hydroxycinnamaldehydes for monolignol/lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux toward lignin-associated phenolic oxidation rather than protective soluble phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; discussed as functionally important for lignin formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Phenolic compounds associated with black spot browning/lignification
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress defense and contribution to enzymatic browning/lignification in peel tissue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in PVC-stored peel than OPP-stored peel at 3 d
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; downregulated in OPP versus PVC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher RsCCR expression is associated with increased lignin-related phenolic oxidation and black spot formation; OPP downregulates RsCCR and delays black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to reduce postharvest black spot by suppressing lignification-associated browning in radish peel
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible effects on structural phenylpropanoid metabolism and defense if strongly reduced
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; metabolomics; physiological browning assessment under contrasting packaging
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsCCR was significantly downregulated in OPP-stored radish peel relative to PVC. 2. Lower RsCCR expression coincided with reduced POD/PPO-associated browning and lower black spot incidence. 3. The study implicates RsCCR-mediated lignin formation in postharvest black spot development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsCCR expression in OPP-3d was 58.85% lower than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Lignin polymerization and phenolic browning module
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cinnamyl alcohols and phenolic compounds with H2O2 as co-substrate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes phenolic substrates using hydrogen peroxide, contributing to lignin polymerization and phenolic browning
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly promotes oxidation/polymerization leading to blackened compounds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; treated as an important browning-associated step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress response but also tissue browning and lignification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in PVC-stored peel; reduced in OPP-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; downregulated in OPP versus PVC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced RsPOD expression under OPP is associated with lower POD activity, less phenolic oxidation, and delayed black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for postharvest management or selection of reduced browning susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Peroxidases also function in defense and ROS metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; enzyme activity assay; metabolomics; phenotype comparison
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsPOD expression was lower in OPP than PVC during storage. 2. POD activity decreased in OPP-packaged radishes and coincided with less browning. 3. RsPOD is implicated as a driver of phenolic oxidation contributing to black spot.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsPOD expression in OPP-3d was 69.69% lower than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for phenolic acids and flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry step of phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Total phenols and downstream flavonoid-related antioxidants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in OPP-stored peel than PVC-stored peel at 3 d
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; induced by OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated RsPAL under OPP is associated with increased phenolic accumulation and enhanced resistance to black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to boost antioxidant phenolics and improve postharvest quality
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Phenylpropanoid reprogramming may affect texture, flavor, or lignification balance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling of total phenols/phenolic acids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsPAL was strongly upregulated in OPP-stored peel versus PVC. 2. Increased RsPAL coincided with higher phenol accumulation. 3. Enhanced phenylpropanoid input is linked to delayed black spot via improved antioxidant capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsPAL expression in OPP-3d was 3.53-fold higher than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Central activation step into hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA pool
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for phenolic acids, lignin, and flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in OPP-stored peel than PVC-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; induced by OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated Rs4CL under OPP is associated with greater phenolic/flavonoid biosynthesis and delayed black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering node for enhanced antioxidant phenylpropanoids in postharvest radish
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux redistribution may alter lignin-related traits
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rs4CL was upregulated in OPP-stored radishes. 2. Its induction paralleled increased phenolic and flavonoid accumulation. 3. Rs4CL contributes to enhanced stress tolerance and reduced black spot under OPP.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rs4CL expression in OPP-3d was 3.27-fold higher than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Caffeic acid and related phenylpropanoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to caffeic acid-related phenylpropanoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports production of antioxidant and lignin-related phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in OPP-stored peel than PVC-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; induced by OPP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased RsCOMT expression in OPP is associated with elevated protective phenolics and delayed black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to modulate phenolic composition for better postharvest stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter phenylpropanoid composition and lignification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsCOMT was significantly induced by OPP packaging. 2. Its upregulation accompanied increased phenylpropanoid-derived metabolites. 3. RsCOMT is part of the protective phenolic remodeling associated with delayed black spot.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsCOMT expression in OPP-3d was 3.22-fold higher than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ferulate-related phenylpropanoid intermediates to support syringyl-type monolignol formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Shapes phenolic composition linked to black spot susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in OPP-stored peel than PVC-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; induced by OPP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RsF5H upregulation under OPP correlates with altered phenolic composition and delayed black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be used to fine-tune phenolic profile for improved postharvest quality
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes in lignin/phenylpropanoid composition may have structural effects
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsF5H was markedly upregulated in OPP-stored peel. 2. OPP also increased sinapoyl alcohol-related metabolite abundance. 3. RsF5H likely contributes to protective phenolic remodeling during black spot delay.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsF5H expression in OPP-3d was 4.17-fold higher than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; functionally important for flavonoid induction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in OPP-stored peel than PVC-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; induced by OPP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong induction of RsDFR under OPP is associated with higher flavonoid accumulation and reduced black spot development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good candidate to elevate postharvest flavonoid antioxidants in radish
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter pigment-related or other flavonoid-dependent traits
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsDFR was highly upregulated in OPP-stored radish peel. 2. OPP also increased flavonoid metabolites such as quercetin, tricetin, kaempferol and catechin. 3. RsDFR induction is linked to improved antioxidant status and delayed black spot.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsDFR expression in OPP-3d was 8.56-fold higher than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in OPP-stored peel than PVC-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; induced by OPP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RsANS upregulation under OPP supports flavonoid accumulation and delayed black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for postharvest enhancement of flavonoid-mediated stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect flavonoid profile and associated quality traits
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsANS was upregulated in OPP-packaged radishes. 2. Its induction coincided with greater flavonoid abundance. 3. RsANS contributes to the antioxidant state associated with reduced black spot.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsANS expression in OPP-3d was 5.12-fold higher than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: AA release and pro-inflammatory oxylipin branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream committed release step for arachidonic acid-derived metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes membrane phospholipids to release arachidonic acid, enabling synthesis of downstream inflammatory oxylipins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool that promotes ROS generation and black spot development
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a limiting/key step in AA, pro-inflammatory substances, and ROS generation according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Arachidonic acid; leukotriene A4; prostaglandin G2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inflammatory/oxidative stress-associated metabolites linked to tissue damage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in PVC-stored peel; lower in OPP-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; suppressed by OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of RsPLA2G under OPP reduces arachidonic acid, LTA4, PGG2, ROS, lipid peroxidation, and delays black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging; influenced by flavonoid and ROS status
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for suppressing postharvest oxidative injury and black spot by limiting AA-derived oxylipin formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible effects on membrane lipid signaling and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; metabolomics of AA/LTA4/PGG2; ROS/MDA/electrolyte leakage measurements; phenotype comparison
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsPLA2G expression was significantly lower in OPP-packaged radishes than in PVC-packaged radishes. 2. Lower RsPLA2G coincided with reduced arachidonic acid, LTA4, PGG2, ROS, and lipid peroxidation. 3. The paper highlights co-regulation of RsPLA2G by flavonoids and ROS as a key mechanism affecting black spot formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central gene highlighted in article highlights and conclusions; expression in OPP-3d was 61.31% lower than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Linoleic acid and alpha-linolenic acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Early oxidation step in unsaturated fatty acid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Associated with maintenance of alpha-linolenic acid metabolism and membrane/stress responses linked to delayed black spot
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane stabilization, stress and disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in OPP-stored peel than PVC-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; increases with storage under OPP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher RsLOX2S expression under OPP is associated with elevated alpha-linolenic acid-related metabolism, membrane stabilization, and delayed black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve postharvest stress tolerance via lipid remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: LOX products can also influence off-flavor or stress signaling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsLOX2S was strongly induced in OPP-stored radishes. 2. OPP also maintained higher alpha-linolenic acid abundance. 3. RsLOX2S-associated lipid metabolism may stabilize membranes and delay black spot.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsLOX2S expression in OPP-3d was 7.96-fold higher than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Early committed step in cuticle monomer formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates terminal carbon of fatty acid-derived cuticle precursors to form omega-hydroxylated monomers for cutin/wax biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursors for cuticle renewal that helps delay black spot
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; presented as an important cuticle synthesis gene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Surface barrier, protection from environmental stress and quality loss
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in OPP-stored peel than PVC-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; induced by OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated RsCYP86A4S under OPP is associated with enhanced cuticle biosynthesis/renewal and delayed black spot onset
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to strengthen cuticle renewal and postharvest disorder resistance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May influence transpiration and surface properties
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsCYP86A4S was strongly upregulated in OPP-packaged radishes. 2. The study links this with enhanced cuticle synthesis gene expression. 3. Cuticle renewal is proposed to contribute to delayed black spot.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsCYP86A4S expression in OPP-3d was 5.42-fold higher than in PVC-3d.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream step in wax monomer synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces long-chain fatty acyl-CoAs to primary alcohols used in cuticular wax formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Provides wax precursors for cuticle renewal associated with delayed black spot
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in OPP-stored peel than PVC-stored peel
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peel during postharvest storage; induced by OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased RsFAR expression under OPP supports wax biosynthesis/cuticle renewal and contributes to delayed black spot
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at 30 °C under PVC versus OPP packaging
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve cuticle-mediated postharvest protection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter transpiration, gloss, or surface lipid composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; integrated omics and physiological interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq); untargeted metabolomics; Supplementary data linked to article DOI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RsFAR was strongly upregulated in OPP-stored radishes. 2. The gene is part of cutin/wax synthesis genes induced by OPP. 3. Enhanced cuticle renewal is proposed to delay black spot development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RsFAR expression in OPP-3d was 5.28-fold higher than in PVC-3d.
